title: Linked List, Stack, and Queue Implementation
summary: Implement a singly linked list and use it to support stack and queue behavior with explicit edge-case handling.
studentInstructions: Create linked_structures.py containing Node, SinglyLinkedList, LinkedStack, and LinkedQueue classes.
SinglyLinkedList must support append, prepend, remove_first, find, __len__, and to_list behavior.
LinkedStack must support push, pop, peek, is_empty, and __len__ behavior.
LinkedQueue must support enqueue, dequeue, peek, is_empty, and __len__ behavior.
Create tests.py with tests for empty structures, one-item structures, multiple-item structures, and removal until empty.
Create implementation_note.md explaining how head and tail references change during key operations.

rubric.description: The linked list correctly implements required operations and maintains accurate head, tail, and length state.
The stack correctly follows last-in, first-out behavior and handles pop and peek on empty structures appropriately.
The queue correctly follows first-in, first-out behavior and maintains head and tail during enqueue and dequeue operations.
The implementation correctly handles empty, one-node, and multi-node cases without losing references or corrupting length.
Tests cover all required structures and operations, including boundary cases and error behavior.
The note clearly explains reference updates for append, prepend, remove_first, push, pop, enqueue, and dequeue.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment and its rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
